data.title: Mastercard Acquires BVNK for $1.8 Billion to Enhance Blockchain Payments Infrastructure
data.description: Mastercard has acquired stablecoin infrastructure startup BVNK for $1.8 billion, marking a significant expansion into blockchain-based payments. According to NS3.AI, this acquisition provides Mastercard with essential infrastructure for cross-border payments, settlement, and liquidity management. The move is part of a broader institutional trend towards stablecoin technology, following a surge in crypto mergers and acquisitions, which reached $37 billion in 2025.

data.title: Jefferies Executives Focus on Declining Stock Amid Celebration
data.description: Jefferies executives convened at a venue on the New Jersey shore to honor a colleague, yet discussions frequently shifted to the bank's declining stock performance. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the persistent concern among the executives regarding the financial institution's stock trajectory. Despite the celebratory occasion, the focus remained on the challenges faced by Jefferies in the current market environment. The gathering underscored the ongoing scrutiny of the bank's financial health and the implications for its future strategies.

data.title: Italian Billionaire's Fintech Conglomerate Buys Bonds Amid Short Seller Pressure
data.description: Italian billionaire Andrea Pignataro's fintech conglomerate has acquired its own bonds at a discount, responding to increased short selling activity against its debt. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns over the future of the software sector, which have led to heightened scrutiny and speculation by investors. The move by Pignataro's company reflects a strategic effort to manage its financial position amid market uncertainties. The acquisition comes as the fintech industry faces challenges, with investors closely monitoring developments in the software sector.

data.title: Former Deutsche Bank Executive Pursues £500 Million in UK Lawsuit
data.description: Michele Faissola, a former executive at Deutsche Bank, is pursuing a lawsuit in the UK seeking up to £500 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Faissola's claims that the bank's actions have caused significant damage to his professional career. The lawsuit underscores the ongoing legal challenges faced by financial institutions and their former executives. Faissola's case adds to the complex legal landscape surrounding Deutsche Bank, which has been involved in various legal disputes over the years. The outcome of this lawsuit could have implications for both Faissola and the bank, as it navigates its legal responsibilities and reputational concerns.

data.title: ECB President Calls for Fiscal Restraint Amid Energy Price Surge
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) President Christine Lagarde has called on governments to exercise fiscal restraint in response to rising energy prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Lagarde's concerns that excessive financial aid to voters could undermine efforts to control inflation. She emphasized the importance of maintaining a balanced approach to fiscal policy to avoid exacerbating economic challenges. Lagarde's remarks come as energy costs continue to climb, posing a significant threat to economic stability across Europe. Her comments underscore the delicate balance policymakers must strike between supporting citizens and ensuring long-term economic health.

data.title: AI TRENDS | World Bank Revises Strategy to Address AI's Impact on Global Workforce
data.description: The World Bank is revising its approach to job creation in response to the potential effects of artificial intelligence on employment. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the organization's efforts to adapt its strategies to support workers in the world's poorest regions. The institution aims to address the challenges posed by AI technologies, which could significantly alter labor markets and employment opportunities. By focusing on these adjustments, the World Bank seeks to ensure sustainable job growth and economic stability in vulnerable areas. The initiative reflects a broader recognition of AI's transformative impact on global economies and the need for proactive measures to mitigate its effects on the workforce.

data.title: Iranian Missile Hits Haifa Refinery, Israeli Media Reports
data.description: An Iranian missile reportedly struck the Haifa refinery in Israel, according to Israeli media sources. The incident did not result in any casualties. According to Jin10, the missile attack highlights ongoing tensions in the region. Further details about the incident and its implications are yet to be disclosed by official sources.

data.title: CVC and Nordic Consider Exit Strategies for Cary
data.description: CVC Capital Partners and Nordic Capital are reportedly in the preliminary phases of evaluating potential exit strategies for Cary, a company specializing in vehicle glass repair. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the situation, that the private equity firms are exploring various options for the business. The discussions are still in the early stages, and no final decisions have been made regarding the future of Cary. The company, known for its expertise in vehicle glass repair, has been under the ownership of CVC and Nordic for some time. As the firms assess their options, the market will be closely watching for any developments in this potential transaction.

data.title: Web3 wallet TopNod surpassed 1 million users in just 170 days since its launch.
data.description: Web3 wallet application TopNod officially announced today that its global user base has surpassed 1 million. Reportedly, the product achieved this milestone in just 170 days since its launch, demonstrating extremely strong market growth momentum. TopNod stated, "1 million is just the starting line. Our core mission is to break down the barriers to Web3 usage, transforming complex blockchain technology into real value that all users can seamlessly experience." Furthermore, the official statement expressed gratitude to early ecosystem partners and hinted at an upcoming major product upgrade.

data.title: U.S. Embassy in Lithuania: Belarus Releases 250 Prisoners with U.S. Mediation
data.description: Belarus has released 250 prisoners following mediation efforts by the United States, according to the U.S. Embassy in Lithuania. This development marks a significant diplomatic achievement in the region. According to Jin10, the release comes amid ongoing international discussions regarding human rights and political freedoms in Belarus. The U.S. has been actively involved in negotiations to address these issues, aiming to improve the situation for those detained under contentious circumstances. The release of these prisoners is seen as a positive step towards easing tensions and fostering dialogue between Belarus and the international community. Further details on the identities of the released prisoners or the conditions of their release have not been disclosed.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Seeks to Raise $10 Billion for Global Lending Fund
data.description:
Goldman Sachs has initiated early discussions with investors to secure a minimum of $10 billion for a global direct lending fund. Bloomberg posted on X, citing individuals familiar with the situation. The move is part of Goldman Sachs' strategy to expand its presence in the direct lending market, which has been gaining traction among institutional investors seeking higher returns.

The fund aims to provide loans directly to companies, bypassing traditional banking channels. This approach allows Goldman Sachs to capitalize on the growing demand for alternative financing solutions. The initiative reflects a broader trend in the financial industry, where direct lending is becoming an increasingly popular investment avenue.

Goldman Sachs' efforts to raise this substantial amount underscore its commitment to strengthening its foothold in the direct lending sector. The bank's strategy aligns with the interests of investors looking for opportunities in a low-interest-rate environment.

The success of this fundraising effort could position Goldman Sachs as a significant player in the global direct lending market, offering a competitive edge in providing tailored financial solutions to businesses worldwide.

data.title: Gundlach Predicts Imminent Fed Rate Hike Based on Treasury Yields
data.description: Jeffrey Gundlach, often referred to as the 'New Bond King,' has indicated that the yield on two-year U.S. Treasury bonds suggests an upcoming interest rate hike by the Federal Reserve. According to ChainCatcher, Gundlach's analysis points to the bond market's anticipation of a shift in monetary policy. This development comes amid ongoing discussions about the Federal Reserve's strategy to manage inflation and economic growth. Gundlach's insights are closely watched by investors and analysts, given his reputation in the bond market.

data.title: U.S. Trade Judge Approves Redesigned Apple Watch in Patent Case
data.description: A U.S. trade judge has approved a redesigned version of the Apple Watch in a patent infringement case. According to Jin10, the decision comes amid ongoing legal disputes over patent rights involving the tech giant. The ruling allows Apple to continue selling its smartwatch with the new design, addressing concerns raised in the patent case. This development is part of a broader legal battle over intellectual property rights in the technology sector. The approval of the redesigned Apple Watch is seen as a significant step for Apple in maintaining its market presence while navigating complex patent issues.

data.title: Electric Truck Sales Decline as KKR-Backed Firm Invests in Charging Market
data.description: Electric truck sales in the United States have decreased, influenced by U.S. President Donald Trump's rollbacks on environmental regulations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite the downturn in sales, a company backed by private equity firm KKR is optimistic about the potential of the electric vehicle charging market. The firm believes that investing in charging infrastructure remains a promising opportunity, even as the broader market faces challenges. This strategic move underscores the confidence in the long-term growth of electric vehicles and the necessary support systems.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Oasis Security Secures $120 Million in Funding
data.description: Oasis Security, a cybersecurity startup specializing in managing system access for non-human accounts such as AI agents, has successfully raised $120 million in a funding round. Bloomberg posted on X that the investment was led by prominent venture capital firms Sequoia and Accel. This funding aims to enhance Oasis Security's capabilities in safeguarding digital environments, particularly as the use of AI agents continues to expand. The company plans to utilize the capital to further develop its technology and expand its market presence.

data.title: Sui Plans to Launch Bitcoin-Backed Lending Platform Hashi
data.description: Sui announced on March 19 its intention to introduce Hashi, a Bitcoin-backed lending platform on the Sui network. According to NS3.AI, Hashi is designed to enable users to use native Bitcoin as collateral for on-chain services. Sui also mentioned that a developer network (devnet) is anticipated to be available soon.

data.title: Hamilton Lane Invests in Republic to Enhance Private Market Access
data.description: Hamilton Lane has strategically invested in Republic, although the financial details of the investment remain undisclosed. According to NS3.AI, the partnership aims to advance tokenization and asset distribution, thereby providing individual investors with broader access to private markets.

data.title: Forward Industries repurchased approximately $27.4 million of its own stock through a Galaxy loan.
data.description: Forward Industries (NASDAQ: FWDI) announced a share buyback of approximately $27.4 million, funded by a short-term loan from Galaxy Digital, as it continues to advance its treasury strategy centered on Solana. The company stated it will repurchase approximately 6.16 million shares from an institutional investor through a private transaction, reducing its outstanding shares by about 7%, which is expected to improve its SOL per share metric. Forward secured a $40 million digital asset-backed loan from Galaxy Digital at an average interest rate of approximately 3.4%, with a term of less than five months, secured by pledged SOL, which yields an annualized return of approximately 6.2%. This buyback comes at a time when both the company's share price and its underlying assets have declined. FWDI's share price is down approximately 25% year-to-date, and SOL is down approximately 30% over the same period. Forward began accumulating SOL shares in September 2025 when the price was around $240; currently, the price is around $88, representing a decline of over 60% from its initial investment. Despite facing shrinkage, Forward continues to expand its holdings, currently holding over 7 million SOL tokens, worth approximately $616 million at current prices, making it the largest known corporate SOL holder. Solana Company ranks second with approximately 2.3 million SOL tokens. Furthermore, Forward expects operating expenses to decline in the coming quarters, with core SG&A costs projected to decrease by approximately 45% between the first and third fiscal quarters.

data.title: Elliott Supports Launch of New Hedge Fund by Credit Specialist Debdeep Maji
data.description: Billionaire Paul Singer's Elliott Management is backing the new hedge fund initiated by credit specialist Debdeep Maji. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this development as part of a broader trend where multistrategy firms are increasingly seeking external expertise to manage substantial assets. Maji's venture represents the latest in a series of strategic moves by Elliott to diversify its investment portfolio and leverage specialized knowledge in credit markets. This approach aligns with the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its investment strategies and optimize returns in a competitive financial landscape.

data.title: High-Leverage Short Position Opened with Significant BTC and ETH Holdings
data.description:
A recent analysis by an on-chain monitoring platform reveals that the address 0x049b opened a 20x leverage short position approximately two hours ago. According to ChainCatcher, this position involves 428.73 BTC and 13,852 ETH, with a combined nominal value of around $58.75 million. The current unrealized profit from this position exceeds $1.15 million.

The liquidation prices for these positions are set at $72,409.98 for BTC and $2,226.62 for ETH.
